---
title: "Asthma Plant (Euphorbia hirta)"
slug: "euphorbia-hirta"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474725496/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Asthma Plant"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Malpighiales"
tax_family: "Euphorbiaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Malpighiales", "Euphorbiaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474725496/medium.jpg" alt="Asthma Plant" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/hoskissbirder">
hoskissbirder</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264275443">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Malpighiales
-   **Family:** Euphorbiaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=131560)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in disturbed areas such as lawns, sidewalks, and roadsides.

## Natural history notes

A low-growing herb characterized by its hairy stems, opposite leaves with a reddish tint, and small clustered flowers. In Florida, it is commonly found in disturbed areas such as lawns, sidewalks, and roadsides. It grows during the warmer months and produces a milky sap typical of spurges, often forming spreading patches in compact or sandy soils.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/131560-Euphorbia-hirta)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/710)
